Here is a paragraph about Grindelia hirta (Gumweed):

Grindelia hirta, also known as Gumweed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Grindelia hirta include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Grindelia hirta is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
